30	
31	According to a semi-canon source, during the [Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion](wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_rebellion) in [261 AC](wiki:261_AC), Ser Amory threw the [last Lord Tarbeck](wiki:Last_Lord_Tarbeck), a three-year-old boy, down a well in [Tarbeck Hall](wiki:Tarbeck_Hall). It is disputed whether this was done per the orders of Ser [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), or without his knowledge.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-10)
32	
33	In [283 AC](wiki:283_AC), Amory was responsible for the murder of the three-year old Princess [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) during the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing). Per the order of Tywin Lannister, Amory and [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) scaled the walls of [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast) to access the royal apartments.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Alongside and in front of several [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) [men-at-arms](wiki:Men-at-arms),(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) he dragged the screaming toddler from under her father [Rhaegar's](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) bed, and when she kicked him, stabbed her dozens of times.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)
34	
35	## Appearances & Description
36	
37	Ser Amory is a stout man with a piggy face,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) little pig's eyes,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2) and a high, thin voice.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)
38	
39	The knight is regarded as a cruel and unintelligent, brutal man.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) He is the sort of man to order the deaths of non-combatants as if it bores him.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3)
40	
41	Amory's [breastplate](wiki:Armor) is decorated with ornate scrollwork,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4) and a [manticore](wiki:Manticore) is depicted on his shield.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) His [warhorse](wiki:Horse) is armored.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)
42	
43	## Narrative Arc
44	
45	### A Game of Thrones
46	
47	At the onset of the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings) in [299 AC](wiki:299_AC), Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) orders his brother, Ser [Kevan](wiki:Kevan_Lannister), to direct Ser Amory, along with his fellow bannerman Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat), to forage the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) and to burn what he does not take. Each of the raider captains is to have three hundred cavalry.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)
48	
49	### A Clash of Kings
50	
51	At a [holdfast](wiki:Holdfast) in [a town](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) by the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye), Amory's raiding party encounters [Yoren](wiki:Yoren)'s group of recruits for the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch), with [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) hidden among them. Even though the Watch takes no part in the wars of the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms), Amory orders his men-at-arms to storm the holdfast,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-6) and Yoren is killed in the [fight at the holdfast](wiki:Fight_at_the_holdfast).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Because of Yoren's fate, Arya adds Amory's name to the [list of people she wants to kill](wiki:Arya_Stark).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) Amory departs the town without burying any of the dead.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)
52	
53	Amory captures two innocent [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk), a beekeeper and his wife, and threatens far and wide to hang them if Lord [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) does not surrender to him. When Beric surrenders, Amory hangs the beekeeper and his wife anyway, with Beric strung up in the middle. Beric is later resurrected by [Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)
54	
55	Arya Stark, disguised as a servant in [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), is offered the deaths of three people by [Jaqen H'ghar](wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar). She considers Amory for one of them, before deciding on [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)
56	
57	When [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) takes the majority of his army east to engage [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), he makes Amory [castellan](wiki:Castellan) of [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), with the [Bloody Mummers](wiki:Bloody_Mummers) also left behind to do the foraging.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) While castellan, Amory resides in the castellan's chambers in [Kingspyre Tower](wiki:Kingspyre_Tower), with only a hundred men left to guard the rest of Harrenhal,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3) and drinks a great deal of the best [wine](wiki:Wine) in the castle's cellars.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)
58	
59	Amory is eventually captured by Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton), after Roose persuades [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) to betray [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) and to seize control of the castle. After the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), Amory is paraded naked before Roose and Vargo. As he pleads and sobs, he is kicked into the [bear](wiki:Bear) pit to be killed by the bear inside it. Arya Stark is present to witness Amory's death, and mentally notes, "The bear is all in black. Like Yoren."(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-4) The bear tears Amory apart.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)
60	
61	### A Storm of Swords
62	
63	[Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) finds a human jawbone in the bear pit of Harrenhal. As it still has maggoty flesh on it, it may have been Amory's.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)
64	
65	Prince [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell) comes to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to seek justice for the deaths of his sister Princess [Elia](wiki:Elia_Martell) and her children, Princess [Rhaenys](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) and Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar)), during the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) tells his son [Tyrion](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) his plan to blame all three of the deaths entirely on the deceased Amory (who in truth had only killed Rhaenys), to circumvent giving Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) over to Oberyn. According to Tywin, Amory justified his brutal murder of Rhaenys because the screaming child had kicked him. Tywin plans to tell Oberyn that Amory acted on his own, without orders, to gain favor from the new king, [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), and hopes that Amory's recent brutal death will assuage Oberyn's need for justice.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-3)
66	
67	Later, to spite his father, Tyrion tells Oberyn that Amory Lorch did kill Rhaenys, but also that Gregor Clegane killed Elia and Aegon.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1)
68	
69	### A Feast for Crows
70	
71	In [Sunspear](wiki:Sunspear), [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell), [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne), tells Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) that his charge, Princess [Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon), may be in danger. When Arys protests that only a beast would hurt a little girl, Doran reminds him of Amory and Rhaenys.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)
72	
73	In [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), Arya Stark sees people peacefully welcoming death at the [House of Black and White](wiki:House_of_Black_and_White), and recalls how Amory had begged in fear when thrown into the bear pit.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)
74	
75	At the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie), Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) reminds [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) about the [curse of Harrenhal](wiki:Curse_of_Harrenhal) and of the deaths of all who have held the castle recently, including Amory Lorch.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)
76	
77	Ser [Roger Hogg](wiki:Roger_Hogg) tells [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) how Amory attacked his towerhouse at [Sow's Horn](wiki:Sow's_Horn) and slaughtered his animals, even though he was sworn to [House Hayford](wiki:House_Hayford), who are sworn to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)
78	
79	### A Dance with Dragons
80	
81	In Sunspear, [Ellaria Sand](wiki:Ellaria_Sand) wishes for the end of the Martell hostilities against House Lannister, as all those who had a hand in the deaths of Elia and her children are dead, including Amory Lorch.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Radwd38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)
82	
83	## Quotes
84	
85	### Quotes by Amory
86	
87	> Young boys and old men die the same.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-7)
88	>
89	> —Amory to [Yoren](wiki:Yoren)
90	
91	### Quotes about Amory
92	
93	> [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) had Ser Amory torn apart by a [bear](wiki:Bear) after the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal). That ought to be sufficiently grisly to appease even [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-4)
94	>
95	> —[Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)
96	
97	> I asked him afterward why it had required half a hundred thrusts to kill [a girl](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) of … two? Three? He said she'd kicked him and would not stop screaming. If Lorch had half the wits the gods gave a turnip, he would have calmed her with a few sweet words and used a soft silk pillow. The blood was in him.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-5)
98	>
99	> —[Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)
100	
101	> Only a beast would harm [a little girl](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1)
102	>
103	> —[Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) to [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell)

## Identity

Amory Lorch is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges

- HOLDS_TITLE: Ser (track_b: Titles)
- HOLDS_TITLE: Castellan (track_b: Titles)
- HOLDS_TITLE: Harrenhal (track_b: Titles)
- SWORN_TO: House Lorch (track_b: Allegiances)
- SWORN_TO: House Lannister (track_b: Allegiances)
- CULTURE_OF: Westermen (track_b: Culture)
- BORN_AT: Westerlands (track_b: Born) [246 AC]
- DIED_AT: Harrenhal (track_b: Died) [299 AC]

## Origins

According to a semi-canon source, during the [Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion](wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_rebellion) in [261 AC](wiki:261_AC), Ser Amory threw the [last Lord Tarbeck](wiki:Last_Lord_Tarbeck), a three-year-old boy, down a well in [Tarbeck Hall](wiki:Tarbeck_Hall). It is disputed whether this was done per the orders of Ser [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), or without his knowledge.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-10)

In [283 AC](wiki:283_AC), Amory was responsible for the murder of the three-year old Princess [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) during the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing). Per the order of Tywin Lannister, Amory and [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) scaled the walls of [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast) to access the royal apartments.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Alongside and in front of several [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) [men-at-arms](wiki:Men-at-arms),(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) he dragged the screaming toddler from under her father [Rhaegar's](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) bed, and when she kicked him, stabbed her dozens of times.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)

## Appearances & Description

Ser Amory is a stout man with a piggy face,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) little pig's eyes,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2) and a high, thin voice.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)

The knight is regarded as a cruel and unintelligent, brutal man.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) He is the sort of man to order the deaths of non-combatants as if it bores him.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3)

Amory's [breastplate](wiki:Armor) is decorated with ornate scrollwork,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4) and a [manticore](wiki:Manticore) is depicted on his shield.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) His [warhorse](wiki:Horse) is armored.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

At the onset of the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings) in [299 AC](wiki:299_AC), Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) orders his brother, Ser [Kevan](wiki:Kevan_Lannister), to direct Ser Amory, along with his fellow bannerman Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat), to forage the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) and to burn what he does not take. Each of the raider captains is to have three hundred cavalry.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

### A Clash of Kings

At a [holdfast](wiki:Holdfast) in [a town](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) by the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye), Amory's raiding party encounters [Yoren](wiki:Yoren)'s group of recruits for the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch), with [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) hidden among them. Even though the Watch takes no part in the wars of the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms), Amory orders his men-at-arms to storm the holdfast,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-6) and Yoren is killed in the [fight at the holdfast](wiki:Fight_at_the_holdfast).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Because of Yoren's fate, Arya adds Amory's name to the [list of people she wants to kill](wiki:Arya_Stark).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) Amory departs the town without burying any of the dead.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)

Amory captures two innocent [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk), a beekeeper and his wife, and threatens far and wide to hang them if Lord [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) does not surrender to him. When Beric surrenders, Amory hangs the beekeeper and his wife anyway, with Beric strung up in the middle. Beric is later resurrected by [Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

Arya Stark, disguised as a servant in [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), is offered the deaths of three people by [Jaqen H'ghar](wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar). She considers Amory for one of them, before deciding on [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)

When [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) takes the majority of his army east to engage [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), he makes Amory [castellan](wiki:Castellan) of [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), with the [Bloody Mummers](wiki:Bloody_Mummers) also left behind to do the foraging.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) While castellan, Amory resides in the castellan's chambers in [Kingspyre Tower](wiki:Kingspyre_Tower), with only a hundred men left to guard the rest of Harrenhal,(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3) and drinks a great deal of the best [wine](wiki:Wine) in the castle's cellars.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

Amory is eventually captured by Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton), after Roose persuades [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) to betray [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) and to seize control of the castle. After the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), Amory is paraded naked before Roose and Vargo. As he pleads and sobs, he is kicked into the [bear](wiki:Bear) pit to be killed by the bear inside it. Arya Stark is present to witness Amory's death, and mentally notes, "The bear is all in black. Like Yoren."(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-4) The bear tears Amory apart.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

### A Storm of Swords

[Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) finds a human jawbone in the bear pit of Harrenhal. As it still has maggoty flesh on it, it may have been Amory's.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

Prince [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell) comes to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to seek justice for the deaths of his sister Princess [Elia](wiki:Elia_Martell) and her children, Princess [Rhaenys](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) and Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar)), during the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) tells his son [Tyrion](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) his plan to blame all three of the deaths entirely on the deceased Amory (who in truth had only killed Rhaenys), to circumvent giving Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) over to Oberyn. According to Tywin, Amory justified his brutal murder of Rhaenys because the screaming child had kicked him. Tywin plans to tell Oberyn that Amory acted on his own, without orders, to gain favor from the new king, [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), and hopes that Amory's recent brutal death will assuage Oberyn's need for justice.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-3)

Later, to spite his father, Tyrion tells Oberyn that Amory Lorch did kill Rhaenys, but also that Gregor Clegane killed Elia and Aegon.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1)

### A Feast for Crows

In [Sunspear](wiki:Sunspear), [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell), [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne), tells Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) that his charge, Princess [Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon), may be in danger. When Arys protests that only a beast would hurt a little girl, Doran reminds him of Amory and Rhaenys.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

In [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), Arya Stark sees people peacefully welcoming death at the [House of Black and White](wiki:House_of_Black_and_White), and recalls how Amory had begged in fear when thrown into the bear pit.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)

At the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie), Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) reminds [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) about the [curse of Harrenhal](wiki:Curse_of_Harrenhal) and of the deaths of all who have held the castle recently, including Amory Lorch.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)

Ser [Roger Hogg](wiki:Roger_Hogg) tells [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) how Amory attacked his towerhouse at [Sow's Horn](wiki:Sow's_Horn) and slaughtered his animals, even though he was sworn to [House Hayford](wiki:House_Hayford), who are sworn to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)

### A Dance with Dragons

In Sunspear, [Ellaria Sand](wiki:Ellaria_Sand) wishes for the end of the Martell hostilities against House Lannister, as all those who had a hand in the deaths of Elia and her children are dead, including Amory Lorch.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Radwd38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)

## Quotes

### Quotes by Amory

> Young boys and old men die the same.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-7)
>
> —Amory to [Yoren](wiki:Yoren)

### Quotes about Amory

> [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) had Ser Amory torn apart by a [bear](wiki:Bear) after the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal). That ought to be sufficiently grisly to appease even [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-4)
>
> —[Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)

> I asked him afterward why it had required half a hundred thrusts to kill [a girl](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) of … two? Three? He said she'd kicked him and would not stop screaming. If Lorch had half the wits the gods gave a turnip, he would have calmed her with a few sweet words and used a soft silk pillow. The blood was in him.(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-5)
>
> —[Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)

> Only a beast would harm [a little girl](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)).(wiki:Amory_Lorch.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1)
>
> —[Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) to [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell)

## Identity

Biter is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges

- SWORN_TO: House Lorch (track_b: Allegiances)
- SWORN_TO: Brave Companions (track_b: Allegiances)
- BORN_AT: Westeros (track_b: Born)

## Origins

According to a semi-canon source, Biter was found as an orphaned child by [Rorge](wiki:Rorge), who raised him ferally to fight in Rorge's [dog](wiki:Dog) and [bear](wiki:Bear) [fighting pit](wiki:Rat_pits) in [Flea Bottom](wiki:Flea_Bottom).(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Rssm1400.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Rssm1401.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

## Appearances & Description

Biter is enormous and bald, with soft, doughy flesh. His tongue has been cut out and he does not speak, except to hiss. His teeth have been filed into points.(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) His weight is enough to make a [courser](wiki:Horse) look half-ready to collapse when riding.(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Clash of Kings

Biter is one of three known criminals imprisoned in the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells) of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Yoren recruits him for the Night's Watch, but considers him so dangerous that he keeps him chained in a [wagon](wiki:Wagon), along with [Jaqen H'ghar](wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar) and [Rorge](wiki:Rorge), for the journey to the [Wall](wiki:Wall).(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

When Yoren's band is [attacked](wiki:Storming_of_the_Gods_Eye_town_holdfast) at the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) by raiders led by Ser [Amory Lorch](wiki:Amory_Lorch), Biter and his companions find themselves trapped in the wagon in the middle of a fire, but [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), before escaping herself, throws an axe into the wagon so that they could break free and save themselves.(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Biter and his companions manage to escape and join Ser Amory's raiding party for a time, eventually arriving at [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal).(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

The three men remain in the garrison holding Harrenhal after [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) departs the castle. They help Arya free the captive [northmen](wiki:North) during the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), though Rorge and Biter do it reluctantly and only because Jaqen requested their help. With Lorch's men slaughtered, Rorge and Biter join the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions).(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

### A Storm of Swords

Rorge tells Jaime that Biter chewed the breasts and nipples off a [septa](wiki:Septa) sometime during their foraging and pillaging as part of the Brave Companions.(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Rasos21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

### A Feast for Crows

After the death of [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat), leader of the Brave Companions, Biter travels with Rorge alongside remnants of the Companions and participates in the [sacking of Saltpans](wiki:Sack_of_Saltpans).

At the [inn at the crossroads](wiki:Inn_at_the_crossroads), after Rorge duels with and is slain by [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth), Biter quickly overpowers Brienne and begins savagely biting her face. He is stopped by [Gendry](wiki:Gendry), who drives a spear through the back of his neck, killing him.(wiki:Biter.cite_ref-Raffc37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

12	---
13	
14	## Identity
15	
16	Jaqen H'ghar is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.
17	
18	## Edges
19	
20	- SWORN_TO: House Lorch (track_b: Allegiances)
21	- SWORN_TO: Brave Companions (track_b: Allegiances)
22	- SWORN_TO: Faceless Men (track_b: Allegiances)
23	- CULTURE_OF: Lorathi (track_b: Culture)
24	- BORN_AT: Essos (track_b: Born)
25	
26	## Origins
27	
28	Jaqen claims he was once of the [Free City](wiki:Free_Cities) of [Lorath](wiki:Lorath),(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) and that his sire is long dead.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2)
29	
30	## Appearances & Description
31	
32	Jaqen is a slender, handsome young man with fine features.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) He has shiny long, straight hair streaming down across his shoulders, colored white on one side and red on the other.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1) Though he speaks the [Common Tongue](wiki:Common_Tongue), he does so in the traditional manner of the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorathi_language), avoiding direct pronouns, referring to himself only as "a man" or "this man" rather than "I" or "me", and to others avoiding "you" and instead using descriptions like "a boy" or "this girl".(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_free_cities:_lorath.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)
33	
34	Jaqen's prisoner's garb is ragged and filthy.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-2) He possesses a thin knife and a cloak, and he is a skilled swordsman.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
35	
36	Jaqen is polite,(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) calm and casual,(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) and always smiles.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5) Serving girls admire him.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-3) His friendly voice(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6) can resemble [silk](wiki:Silk) and [steel](wiki:Steel).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) thinks that the intimidating [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) might be scared of Jaqen.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)
37	
38	## Narrative Arc
39	
40	### A Game of Thrones
41	
42	At the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), [Yoren](wiki:Yoren) pleads for volunteers to join the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch). No [knights](wiki:Knight) accept the [wandering crow](wiki:Wandering_crow)'s offer, however, so Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), gives Yoren his pick of the king's dungeons.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Ragot44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Jaqen is one of the three common men Yoren takes from the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells),(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) the others being [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)
43	
44	### A Clash of Kings
45	
46	When Yoren's party departs [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) for the [Wall](wiki:Wall), he keeps Rorge, Biter, and Jaqen fettered in a cage in a wagon. Despite the situation, Jaqen is polite to [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), who is disguised as a boy named "Arry".(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)
47	
48	Jaqen remains caged with Biter and Rorge when the group of recruits decides to spend the night in the [holdfast](wiki:Holdfast) of [a deserted town](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) on the southern shore of the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye). The town is [burned down](wiki:Storming_of_the_Gods_Eye_town_holdfast) that night by men under the command of Ser [Amory Lorch](wiki:Amory_Lorch). As the cage is threatened by the fire, Jaqen shouts for Arya's help, claiming he can fight. Before Arya flees into a tunnel, she throws an axe to Rorge, who uses it to chop through the wagon, allowing the three prisoners to escape the fire.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)
49	
50	Rorge, Biter, and Jaqen join Ser Amory's raiding party, and Arya, now disguised as the servant girl "Weasel", observes their arrival at [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), feeling betrayed. However, that night Jaqen interrupts Arya's sleep, and explains that he knew she is actually a girl. Jaqen insists that because Arya had helped him, Rorge, and Biter escape death by burning at the Gods Eye, he now owes her three deaths to repay those she stole from the "[Red God](wiki:R'hllor)". Jaqen asks for her to give him three names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-4)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rssm6539.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)
51	
52	After Arya overhears [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck) tell the story of Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [Mountain's men](wiki:Mountain's_men) raping [Layna](wiki:Layna), a brewer's daughter, she gives Chiswyck's name to Jaqen. Three days later, Arya learns that Chiswyck has broken his neck after falling from a wallwalk; some claim that the soldier was killed by the ghost of [Harren the Black](wiki:Harren_Hoare).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-5)
53	
54	Later on, [Weese](wiki:Weese), the understeward of the [Wailing Tower](wiki:Wailing_Tower), slaps and berates "Weasel". Arya finds Jaqen relaxing in Harrenhal's bathhouse, and whispers Weese's name to the Lorathi. The next day, Arya regrets her choice while watching the powerful Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) depart Harrenhal with his army. Arya rushes to find Jaqen in an attempt to change her second name, but she discovers that Weese's throat has already been torn out by his own [spotted dog](wiki:Spotted_dog). [Amabel](wiki:Amabel) attributes Weese's death to Harren's ghost.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3)
55	
56	[Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) and the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions) bring to Harrenhal captured [northmen](wiki:North) and [rivermen](wiki:Riverlands) led by [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover) and Ser [Aenys Frey](wiki:Aenys_Frey). Jaqen finds Arya in the [godswood of Harrenhal](wiki:Godswood_of_Harrenhal) and asks her for the third name, claiming he will kill anyone in the [world](wiki:World) that she names, even his own father if he were still alive, even King [Joffrey I Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_I_Baratheon). After Jaqen refuses to aid Arya in freeing the captives, she shocks him by whispering *"Jaqen H'ghar"* in his ear. Arya promises to unsay his name only if he helps her, so Jaqen sends the girl to the kitchens to have hot broth prepared. Rorge and Biter then help Jaqen and Arya bring four kettles to the dungeon beneath the [Widow's Tower](wiki:Widow's_Tower). Eight of Amory's guardsmen prepare to eat the broth, but the sellswords throw the scalding broth in their faces and begin killing them. One guard draws his sword, but Jaqen duels him with his own sword and stabs the man in the heart. When Robett Glover asks if the three men are Brave Companions, Rorge replies affirmatively.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)
57	
58	Rorge and Biter go off to help the freed northmen and Brave Companions with the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), but Jaqen remains by Arya's side. She takes back his name and asks if she still has a third death to request, but Jaqen reminds her of the various men he has already helped kill on her behalf. Jaqen informs her it is time for him to "die", passing his hand down his face and changing his appearance. Jaqen's cheeks become fuller, his eyes are closer together, his nose is now hooked, and a scar appears on his right cheek. Jaqen's long red and white hair is changed into a cap of tight black curls. He also now has a shiny [golden](wiki:Gold) tooth. When Arya requests that he teach her how to do that, the man who was once Jaqen, now speaking with typical Westeros pronoun usage, tells her that she must come with him across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea). When she says she cannot go, he gives Arya a small [iron](wiki:Iron) [coin](wiki:Currency), claiming it has great value. He says that should she ever wish to find him again, she should give the coin to any man from [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), and tell him "*[valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*". When Arya asks him once more to stay, he tells her Jaqen is dead, and he has promises to keep. He then departs Harrenhal during the fighting, and Vargo's Brave Companions turn the castle over to Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-4)
59	
60	Although Jaqen did not explain the meaning of *valar morghulis*, Arya begins to use the words with [her prayer listing the people she hates and wants dead](wiki:Arya_Stark).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-5)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) Arya sometimes regrets not leaving with Jaqen. As she examines the worn iron coin he gave her, which has foreign writing on one side and a man whose face has rubbed off on the other, Arya becomes so angry at the lies Jaqen told her that she throws the coin away, but an hour later she decides to find it again and keep it. When Arya escapes Harrenhal, she distracts a [Bolton guard](wiki:House_Bolton_guards) with the iron coin and then slits his throat; she whispers *"valar morghulis"* as he dies.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1)
61	
62	### A Storm of Swords
63	
64	Arya continues to use the words *valar morghulis* to end her nightly prayer.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) When Arya is captured by the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) and meets [Harwin](wiki:Harwin), a former [household guard](wiki:House_Stark_guards) of Winterfell, she tells him much of her adventures since she escaped King's Landing, but does not tell him about how Jaqen and how he killed for her.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-1) She feels strong remembering how Jaqen made her the ghost in Harrenhal, but is unhappy recalling how he and so many others have left her.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-1)
65	
66	Seeking passage from [Westeros](wiki:Westeros), Arya shows the iron coin to [Ternesio Terys](wiki:Ternesio_Terys), the captain of the Braavosi ship *[Titan's Daughter](wiki:Titan's_Daughter)*. At the captain's confusion, she recalls that Jaqen had also given her words to say. When Arya says, "*[Valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*", Ternesio replies, "*[Valar dohaeris](wiki:Valar_dohaeris)*", and agrees to transport her from [Saltpans](wiki:Saltpans) to Braavos.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos74.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0)
67	
68	### A Feast for Crows
69	
70	When Arya arrives at the [House of Black and White](wiki:House_of_Black_and_White) in Braavos, she offers the iron coin to a [kindly man](wiki:Kindly_man), who says he does not know Jaqen's name.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) Arya is allowed to serve at the temple, but the kindly man continues to not recognize Jaqen's name. The kindly man eventually tells her about the origins of the [Faceless Men](wiki:Faceless_Men), and she learns that they do not use names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0)
71	
72	A man claiming to be an [alchemist](wiki:Alchemist), whose appearance matches the appearance of Jaqen's new face, approaches [Pate](wiki:Pate_(novice)), a novice of the [Citadel](wiki:Citadel), in [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown). He promises Pate a [gold dragon](wiki:Currency) in exchange for Archmaester [Walgrave](wiki:Walgrave)'s key. They make the exchange, but Pate dies after biting the coin.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffcprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_26-0) When [Samwell Tarly](wiki:Samwell_Tarly) later arrives at the Citadel, he is welcomed by a novice who introduces himself as Pate.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_27-0)
73	
74	### A Dance with Dragons
75	
76	At the House of Black and White, [plague face](wiki:Plague_face) explains to Arya that in order for her to truly become a servant of the [Many-Faced God](wiki:Many-Faced_God), she must be willing to surrender all of her identity, including her name and face. When the kindly man uses the Faceless Men's [magic](wiki:Magic) to give her the face of an ugly, broken girl, Arya passes her hand down her face as she had seen Jaqen do to change his, but nothing apparently happens.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Radwd64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-0)
77	
78	## Quotes
79	
80	### Quotes by Jaqen
81	
82	> A man could use another taste of [beer](wiki:Beer). A man has a thirst, wearing these heavy bracelets. A man could use a bath too. A boy could make a friend.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10)
83	>
84	> —Jaqen, to ["Arry"](wiki:Arya_Stark)
85	
86	> A man must be ashamed of the company he keeps, Arry. This man has the honor to be Jaqen H'ghar, once of the [Free City](wiki:Free_City) of [Lorath](wiki:Lorath). Would that he were home.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11)
87	>
88	> —Jaqen, to ["Arry"](wiki:Arya_Stark)
89	
90	> **Jaqen**: A boy becomes a girl.
91	>
92	> **Arya**: I was always a girl. I didn't think you saw me.
93	>
94	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
95	
96	> **Jaqen**: A man pays his debts. A man owes three.
97	>
98	> **Arya**: Three?
99	>
100	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
101	
102	> **Arya**: How'd you make [the dog](wiki:Spotted_dog) kill [Weese](wiki:Weese)? Did you call [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter) up from [hell](wiki:Seven_hells)? Is Jaqen H'ghar your true name? **Jaqen**: Some men have many names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-6)
103	>
104	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
105	
106	> **Jaqen**: A girl whispers if she fears to speak aloud. Whisper it now. Is it [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon)? **Arya**: It's Jaqen H'ghar.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-7)
107	>
108	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
109	
110	> **Jaqen**: A [god](wiki:God) has his due. And now a man must die.
111	>
112	> **Arya**: Die? But I unsaid the name. You don't need to die now.
113	>
114	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
115	
116	> **Arya**: Who are you? How did you do that? Was it hard? **Jaqen**: No harder than taking a new name, if you know the way.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-9)
117	>
118	> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and Jaqen
119	
120	> **Jaqen**: If you would learn, you must come with me.
121	>
122	> **Arya**: Where? **Jaqen**: Far and away, across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea). **Arya**: I can't. I have to go home. To [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell).
123	>
124	> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
125	
126	> **Arya**: Please don't go, Jaqen. **Jaqen**: Jaqen is as dead as Arry, and I have promises to keep. *[Valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*, Arya Stark.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-11)
127	>
128	> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and Jaqen
129	
130	### Quotes about Jaqen
131	
132	> Something about the way he talked reminded her of [Syrio](wiki:Syrio_Forel); it was the same, yet different too.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-12)
133	>
134	> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
135	
136	> Jaqen made me brave again. He made me a ghost instead of a mouse.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-12)
137	>
138	> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
139	
140	> She had been avoiding the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorath) since [Weese](wiki:Weese)'s death. [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck) had been easy, anyone could push a man off the wallwalk, but Weese had raised that ugly [spotted dog](wiki:Spotted_dog) from a pup, and only some [dark magic](wiki:Sorcery) could have turned the animal against him. [Yoren](wiki:Yoren) found Jaqen in a [black cell](wiki:Black_cells), the same as [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter), she remembered. Jaqen did something horrible and Yoren knew, that's why he kept him in chains. If the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorath) was a [wizard](wiki:Wizard), Rorge and Biter could be [demons](wiki:Demons) he called up from some [hell](wiki:Seven_hells), not men at all.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-13)
141	>
142	> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
143	
144	> Jaqen still owed her one death. In [Old Nan](wiki:Old_Nan)'s stories about men who were given [magic](wiki:Magic) wishes by a [grumkin](wiki:Grumkin), you had to be especially careful with the third wish, because it was the last. […] So long as she could kill with a whisper, Arya need not be afraid of anyone... but once she used up the last death, she would only be a mouse again.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-14)
145	>
146	> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
147	
148	> Sometimes she wished she had gone off across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) with Jaqen H'ghar. She still had the stupid [coin](wiki:Currency) he'd given her, a piece of [iron](wiki:Iron) no larger than a penny and rusted along the rim. One side had writing on it, queer words she could not read. The other showed a man's head, but so worn that all his features had rubbed off. He said it was of great value, but that was probably a lie too, like his name and even his face.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-2)
149	>
150	> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
151	
152	> **Arya**: Is Jaqen H'ghar a priest too? Do you know if Jaqen will be coming back to [Braavos](wiki:Braavos)?
153	>
154	> **Kindly man**: Who? **Arya**: Jaqen H'ghar. He gave me the [iron](wiki:Iron) coin.
155	>
156	> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and the [kindly man](wiki:Kindly_man)
157

file.content:
---
name: "Jaqen H'ghar"
type: character.human
slug: jaqen-hghar
aliases: ["Lorath"]
confidence: tier-1
wiki_source: "https://awoiaf.westeros.org/index.php/Jaqen_H'ghar"
bucket_id: characters-house-lorch

node_version: 1

---

## Identity

Jaqen H'ghar is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges

- SWORN_TO: House Lorch (track_b: Allegiances)
- SWORN_TO: Brave Companions (track_b: Allegiances)
- SWORN_TO: Faceless Men (track_b: Allegiances)
- CULTURE_OF: Lorathi (track_b: Culture)
- BORN_AT: Essos (track_b: Born)

## Origins

Jaqen claims he was once of the [Free City](wiki:Free_Cities) of [Lorath](wiki:Lorath),(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) and that his sire is long dead.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2)

## Appearances & Description

Jaqen is a slender, handsome young man with fine features.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) He has shiny long, straight hair streaming down across his shoulders, colored white on one side and red on the other.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1) Though he speaks the [Common Tongue](wiki:Common_Tongue), he does so in the traditional manner of the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorathi_language), avoiding direct pronouns, referring to himself only as "a man" or "this man" rather than "I" or "me", and to others avoiding "you" and instead using descriptions like "a boy" or "this girl".(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_free_cities:_lorath.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

Jaqen's prisoner's garb is ragged and filthy.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-2) He possesses a thin knife and a cloak, and he is a skilled swordsman.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

Jaqen is polite,(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) calm and casual,(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) and always smiles.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5) Serving girls admire him.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-3) His friendly voice(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6) can resemble [silk](wiki:Silk) and [steel](wiki:Steel).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) thinks that the intimidating [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) might be scared of Jaqen.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

At the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), [Yoren](wiki:Yoren) pleads for volunteers to join the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch). No [knights](wiki:Knight) accept the [wandering crow](wiki:Wandering_crow)'s offer, however, so Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), gives Yoren his pick of the king's dungeons.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Ragot44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Jaqen is one of the three common men Yoren takes from the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells),(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) the others being [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)

### A Clash of Kings

When Yoren's party departs [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) for the [Wall](wiki:Wall), he keeps Rorge, Biter, and Jaqen fettered in a cage in a wagon. Despite the situation, Jaqen is polite to [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), who is disguised as a boy named "Arry".(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)

Jaqen remains caged with Biter and Rorge when the group of recruits decides to spend the night in the [holdfast](wiki:Holdfast) of [a deserted town](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) on the southern shore of the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye). The town is [burned down](wiki:Storming_of_the_Gods_Eye_town_holdfast) that night by men under the command of Ser [Amory Lorch](wiki:Amory_Lorch). As the cage is threatened by the fire, Jaqen shouts for Arya's help, claiming he can fight. Before Arya flees into a tunnel, she throws an axe to Rorge, who uses it to chop through the wagon, allowing the three prisoners to escape the fire.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

Rorge, Biter, and Jaqen join Ser Amory's raiding party, and Arya, now disguised as the servant girl "Weasel", observes their arrival at [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), feeling betrayed. However, that night Jaqen interrupts Arya's sleep, and explains that he knew she is actually a girl. Jaqen insists that because Arya had helped him, Rorge, and Biter escape death by burning at the Gods Eye, he now owes her three deaths to repay those she stole from the "[Red God](wiki:R'hllor)". Jaqen asks for her to give him three names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-4)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rssm6539.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)

After Arya overhears [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck) tell the story of Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [Mountain's men](wiki:Mountain's_men) raping [Layna](wiki:Layna), a brewer's daughter, she gives Chiswyck's name to Jaqen. Three days later, Arya learns that Chiswyck has broken his neck after falling from a wallwalk; some claim that the soldier was killed by the ghost of [Harren the Black](wiki:Harren_Hoare).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-5)

Later on, [Weese](wiki:Weese), the understeward of the [Wailing Tower](wiki:Wailing_Tower), slaps and berates "Weasel". Arya finds Jaqen relaxing in Harrenhal's bathhouse, and whispers Weese's name to the Lorathi. The next day, Arya regrets her choice while watching the powerful Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) depart Harrenhal with his army. Arya rushes to find Jaqen in an attempt to change her second name, but she discovers that Weese's throat has already been torn out by his own [spotted dog](wiki:Spotted_dog). [Amabel](wiki:Amabel) attributes Weese's death to Harren's ghost.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3)

[Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) and the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions) bring to Harrenhal captured [northmen](wiki:North) and [rivermen](wiki:Riverlands) led by [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover) and Ser [Aenys Frey](wiki:Aenys_Frey). Jaqen finds Arya in the [godswood of Harrenhal](wiki:Godswood_of_Harrenhal) and asks her for the third name, claiming he will kill anyone in the [world](wiki:World) that she names, even his own father if he were still alive, even King [Joffrey I Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_I_Baratheon). After Jaqen refuses to aid Arya in freeing the captives, she shocks him by whispering *"Jaqen H'ghar"* in his ear. Arya promises to unsay his name only if he helps her, so Jaqen sends the girl to the kitchens to have hot broth prepared. Rorge and Biter then help Jaqen and Arya bring four kettles to the dungeon beneath the [Widow's Tower](wiki:Widow's_Tower). Eight of Amory's guardsmen prepare to eat the broth, but the sellswords throw the scalding broth in their faces and begin killing them. One guard draws his sword, but Jaqen duels him with his own sword and stabs the man in the heart. When Robett Glover asks if the three men are Brave Companions, Rorge replies affirmatively.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)

Rorge and Biter go off to help the freed northmen and Brave Companions with the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), but Jaqen remains by Arya's side. She takes back his name and asks if she still has a third death to request, but Jaqen reminds her of the various men he has already helped kill on her behalf. Jaqen informs her it is time for him to "die", passing his hand down his face and changing his appearance. Jaqen's cheeks become fuller, his eyes are closer together, his nose is now hooked, and a scar appears on his right cheek. Jaqen's long red and white hair is changed into a cap of tight black curls. He also now has a shiny [golden](wiki:Gold) tooth. When Arya requests that he teach her how to do that, the man who was once Jaqen, now speaking with typical Westeros pronoun usage, tells her that she must come with him across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea). When she says she cannot go, he gives Arya a small [iron](wiki:Iron) [coin](wiki:Currency), claiming it has great value. He says that should she ever wish to find him again, she should give the coin to any man from [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), and tell him "*[valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*". When Arya asks him once more to stay, he tells her Jaqen is dead, and he has promises to keep. He then departs Harrenhal during the fighting, and Vargo's Brave Companions turn the castle over to Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-4)

Although Jaqen did not explain the meaning of *valar morghulis*, Arya begins to use the words with [her prayer listing the people she hates and wants dead](wiki:Arya_Stark).(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-5)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) Arya sometimes regrets not leaving with Jaqen. As she examines the worn iron coin he gave her, which has foreign writing on one side and a man whose face has rubbed off on the other, Arya becomes so angry at the lies Jaqen told her that she throws the coin away, but an hour later she decides to find it again and keep it. When Arya escapes Harrenhal, she distracts a [Bolton guard](wiki:House_Bolton_guards) with the iron coin and then slits his throat; she whispers *"valar morghulis"* as he dies.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1)

### A Storm of Swords

Arya continues to use the words *valar morghulis* to end her nightly prayer.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) When Arya is captured by the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) and meets [Harwin](wiki:Harwin), a former [household guard](wiki:House_Stark_guards) of Winterfell, she tells him much of her adventures since she escaped King's Landing, but does not tell him about how Jaqen and how he killed for her.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-1) She feels strong remembering how Jaqen made her the ghost in Harrenhal, but is unhappy recalling how he and so many others have left her.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-1)

Seeking passage from [Westeros](wiki:Westeros), Arya shows the iron coin to [Ternesio Terys](wiki:Ternesio_Terys), the captain of the Braavosi ship *[Titan's Daughter](wiki:Titan's_Daughter)*. At the captain's confusion, she recalls that Jaqen had also given her words to say. When Arya says, "*[Valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*", Ternesio replies, "*[Valar dohaeris](wiki:Valar_dohaeris)*", and agrees to transport her from [Saltpans](wiki:Saltpans) to Braavos.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Rasos74.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0)

### A Feast for Crows

When Arya arrives at the [House of Black and White](wiki:House_of_Black_and_White) in Braavos, she offers the iron coin to a [kindly man](wiki:Kindly_man), who says he does not know Jaqen's name.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) Arya is allowed to serve at the temple, but the kindly man continues to not recognize Jaqen's name. The kindly man eventually tells her about the origins of the [Faceless Men](wiki:Faceless_Men), and she learns that they do not use names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0)

A man claiming to be an [alchemist](wiki:Alchemist), whose appearance matches the appearance of Jaqen's new face, approaches [Pate](wiki:Pate_(novice)), a novice of the [Citadel](wiki:Citadel), in [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown). He promises Pate a [gold dragon](wiki:Currency) in exchange for Archmaester [Walgrave](wiki:Walgrave)'s key. They make the exchange, but Pate dies after biting the coin.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffcprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_26-0) When [Samwell Tarly](wiki:Samwell_Tarly) later arrives at the Citadel, he is welcomed by a novice who introduces himself as Pate.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Raffc45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_27-0)

### A Dance with Dragons

At the House of Black and White, [plague face](wiki:Plague_face) explains to Arya that in order for her to truly become a servant of the [Many-Faced God](wiki:Many-Faced_God), she must be willing to surrender all of her identity, including her name and face. When the kindly man uses the Faceless Men's [magic](wiki:Magic) to give her the face of an ugly, broken girl, Arya passes her hand down her face as she had seen Jaqen do to change his, but nothing apparently happens.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Radwd64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-0)

## Quotes

### Quotes by Jaqen

> A man could use another taste of [beer](wiki:Beer). A man has a thirst, wearing these heavy bracelets. A man could use a bath too. A boy could make a friend.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10)
>
> —Jaqen, to ["Arry"](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> A man must be ashamed of the company he keeps, Arry. This man has the honor to be Jaqen H'ghar, once of the [Free City](wiki:Free_City) of [Lorath](wiki:Lorath). Would that he were home.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11)
>
> —Jaqen, to ["Arry"](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Jaqen**: A boy becomes a girl.
>
> **Arya**: I was always a girl. I didn't think you saw me.
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Jaqen**: A man pays his debts. A man owes three.
>
> **Arya**: Three?
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Arya**: How'd you make [the dog](wiki:Spotted_dog) kill [Weese](wiki:Weese)? Did you call [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter) up from [hell](wiki:Seven_hells)? Is Jaqen H'ghar your true name? **Jaqen**: Some men have many names.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-6)
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Jaqen**: A girl whispers if she fears to speak aloud. Whisper it now. Is it [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon)? **Arya**: It's Jaqen H'ghar.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-7)
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Jaqen**: A [god](wiki:God) has his due. And now a man must die.
>
> **Arya**: Die? But I unsaid the name. You don't need to die now.
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Arya**: Who are you? How did you do that? Was it hard? **Jaqen**: No harder than taking a new name, if you know the way.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-9)
>
> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and Jaqen

> **Jaqen**: If you would learn, you must come with me.
>
> **Arya**: Where? **Jaqen**: Far and away, across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea). **Arya**: I can't. I have to go home. To [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell).
>
> —Jaqen and [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Arya**: Please don't go, Jaqen. **Jaqen**: Jaqen is as dead as Arry, and I have promises to keep. *[Valar morghulis](wiki:Valar_morghulis)*, Arya Stark.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-11)
>
> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and Jaqen

### Quotes about Jaqen

> Something about the way he talked reminded her of [Syrio](wiki:Syrio_Forel); it was the same, yet different too.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-12)
>
> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> Jaqen made me brave again. He made me a ghost instead of a mouse.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-12)
>
> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> She had been avoiding the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorath) since [Weese](wiki:Weese)'s death. [Chiswyck](wiki:Chiswyck) had been easy, anyone could push a man off the wallwalk, but Weese had raised that ugly [spotted dog](wiki:Spotted_dog) from a pup, and only some [dark magic](wiki:Sorcery) could have turned the animal against him. [Yoren](wiki:Yoren) found Jaqen in a [black cell](wiki:Black_cells), the same as [Rorge](wiki:Rorge) and [Biter](wiki:Biter), she remembered. Jaqen did something horrible and Yoren knew, that's why he kept him in chains. If the [Lorathi](wiki:Lorath) was a [wizard](wiki:Wizard), Rorge and Biter could be [demons](wiki:Demons) he called up from some [hell](wiki:Seven_hells), not men at all.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-13)
>
> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> Jaqen still owed her one death. In [Old Nan](wiki:Old_Nan)'s stories about men who were given [magic](wiki:Magic) wishes by a [grumkin](wiki:Grumkin), you had to be especially careful with the third wish, because it was the last. […] So long as she could kill with a whisper, Arya need not be afraid of anyone... but once she used up the last death, she would only be a mouse again.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-14)
>
> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> Sometimes she wished she had gone off across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) with Jaqen H'ghar. She still had the stupid [coin](wiki:Currency) he'd given her, a piece of [iron](wiki:Iron) no larger than a penny and rusted along the rim. One side had writing on it, queer words she could not read. The other showed a man's head, but so worn that all his features had rubbed off. He said it was of great value, but that was probably a lie too, like his name and even his face.(wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-2)
>
> —thoughts of [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)

> **Arya**: Is Jaqen H'ghar a priest too? Do you know if Jaqen will be coming back to [Braavos](wiki:Braavos)?
>
> **Kindly man**: Who? **Arya**: Jaqen H'ghar. He gave me the [iron](wiki:Iron) coin.
>
> —[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and the [kindly man](wiki:Kindly_man)

He fought in the [battle at the Great Fork](wiki:Battle_at_the_Great_Fork) alongside Lord [Rupert Falwell](wiki:Rupert_Falwell) and other knights from the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) and [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands), as well as hundreds of [Poor Fellows](wiki:Poor_Fellows), against the royal army led by King [Maegor I Targaryen](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen) himself. 13	
14	## Identity
15	
16	Rorge is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.
17	
18	## Edges
19	
20	- SWORN_TO: House Lorch (track_b: Allegiances)
21	- SWORN_TO: Brave Companions (track_b: Allegiances)
22	- CULTURE_OF: Westerosi (track_b: Culture)
23	- BORN_AT: Westeros (track_b: Born)
24	
25	## Origins
26	
27	According to a semi-canon source, Rorge owned a [pot shop](wiki:Pot_shop) or tavern in [Flea Bottom](wiki:Flea_Bottom), that also served as a [rat pit](wiki:Rat_pit) where he would stage rat fights, [dog](wiki:Dog) fights, and [bear](wiki:Bear) cub fights for his profit. At some point, he found the orphan child [Biter](wiki:Biter), and raised him ferally to compete in these fights.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rssm1400.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rssm1401.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)
28	
29	## Appearances & Description
30	
31	Rorge is described as squat, bulky, and hairy, with black hair covering most of his body. He is also described as thick and powerful.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) He is missing his nose, and the nasal bar of the black iron half-helm he comes to wear does not completely conceal the hole.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
32	
33	## Narrative Arc
34	
35	### A Clash of Kings
36	
37	Rorge is one of three known criminals imprisoned in the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells) of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Yoren recruits him for the Night's Watch, but considers him so dangerous that he keeps him chained in a [wagon](wiki:Wagon), along with [Jaqen H'ghar](wiki:Jaqen_H'ghar) and [Biter](wiki:Biter), for the journey to the [Wall](wiki:Wall).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Racok1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
38	
39	When Yoren's band is [attacked](wiki:Storming_of_the_Gods_Eye_town_holdfast) at the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye_town) by raiders led by Ser [Amory Lorch](wiki:Amory_Lorch), Rorge and his companions find themselves trapped in the wagon in the middle of a fire, but [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark), before escaping herself, throws an axe into the wagon so that they could break free and save themselves.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Racok14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Rorge and his companions manage to escape and join Ser Amory's raiding party for a time, eventually arriving at [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Racok30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)
40	
41	The three men remain in the garrison holding Harrenhal after [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) departs the castle. They help Arya free the captive [northmen](wiki:North) during the [fall of Harrenhal](wiki:Fall_of_Harrenhal), though Rorge and Biter do it reluctantly and only because Jaqen requested their help. With Lorch's men slaughtered, Rorge and Biter join the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Racok47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)
42	
43	### A Storm of Swords
44	
45	Rorge is part of the foraging company who captures [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) and [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth) near [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool). He immediately threatens Brienne with rape and severely beats Jaime into submission.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2) He later claims the bloodstained surcoat off the corpse of [Cleos Frey](wiki:Cleos_Frey).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)
46	
47	On the way back to [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), Rorge, [Shagwell](wiki:Shagwell), and [Zollo](wiki:Zollo) attempt to rape Brienne. He argues with Zollo over who would rape Brienne first, but Jaime thwarts them. Jaime considers Rorge one of the three worst members of the Brave Companions.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
48	
49	Rorge is one of the many Brave Companions who remain behind at Harrenhal after several failed foraging runs. He is present when Brienne is thrown into the bear pit and threatens Jaime once more when he comes back to save her, but [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) orders him to stand down.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)
50	
51	### A Feast for Crows
52	
53	After the [capture of Harrenhal](wiki:Capture_of_Harrenhal) and Vargo's death, Rorge went to the [inn at the crossroads](wiki:Inn_at_the_crossroads) with [Shagwell](wiki:Shagwell), [Timeon](wiki:Timeon), [Pyg](wiki:Pyg) and other former [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions). Rorge killed the innkeeper. Then they parted: Shagwell, Timeon, and Pyg went to [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool), while Rorge travelled toward the town of [Saltpans](wiki:Saltpans) with [Biter](wiki:Biter) and other former Brave Companions. (wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Along the way Rorge finds and dons the [hound](wiki:Hound) [helm](wiki:Helm) once worn by [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane). He wears the helm during the brutal [raid on Saltpans](wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans), which results in Sandor being blamed for the attack.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
54	
55	Rorge encounters [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth) at the inn at the crossroads. Brienne goads him by stating, "[Shagwell](wiki:Shagwell) said they [cut your manhood off](wiki:Eunuch) when they took your nose." After Brienne slays Rorge in their duel, she is attacked by the savage Biter and then rescued by [Gendry](wiki:Gendry).(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1)
56	
57	When the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) returns to the crossroads inn, the dog helm is taken from Rorge's body by [Lem Lemoncloak](wiki:Lem), who becomes the new Hound.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)
58	
59	## Quotes
60	
61	> Come closer, and I'll shove that stick up your bunghole and fuck you bloody.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Racok5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)
62	>
63	> —Rorge, to [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark)
64	
65	> You're the ugliest woman I've ever seen, but don't think I can’t make you uglier. You want a nose like mine? Fight me, and you'll get one. And two eyes, that's too many. One scream out o' you, and I'll pop one out and make you eat it, and then I'll pull your fucking teeth out one by one.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Rasos31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1)
66	>
67	> —Rorge, to [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth)
68	
69	> **Brienne**: We only seek one [outlaw](wiki:Outlaw). The [Hound](wiki:Hound). **Meribald**: So Ser [Hyle](wiki:Hyle_Hunt) told me. May [the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven) save you, child. It's said he leaves a trail of [butchered babes and ravished maids](wiki:Raid_on_Saltpans) behind him. The Mad Dog of [Saltpans](wiki:Saltpans), I have heard him called.(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)
70	>
71	> —[Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth) and [Meribald](wiki:Meribald)

(wiki:Rorge.cite_ref-Raffc20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Along the way Rorge finds and dons the [hound](wiki:Hound) [helm](wiki:Helm) once worn by [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane). content.content:
1	# The Weirwood Network — Schema Reference
2	
3	> **This file is the schema reference for all agents working on this project.** It defines entity types, edge types, confidence tiers, file naming conventions, and spoiler gating. Every agent should load this file to understand the data model.
4	>
5	> For project overview, directory structure, and pipeline sequence, see `CLAUDE.md`.
6	> For current project state and progress, see `worklog.md`.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## File Naming Conventions
11	
12	### Chapter Source Files
13	`{book-abbrev}-{pov-character}-{chapter-number}.md`
14	
15	Book abbreviations: `agot`, `acok`, `asos`, `affc`, `adwd`
16	
17	Novella codes (Tales of Dunk and Egg): `thk` (The Hedge Knight), `tss` (The Sworn Sword), `tmk` (The Mystery Knight)
18	
19	Examples:
20	- `agot-bran-01.md` (Bran's first chapter in AGOT)
21	- `asos-catelyn-07.md` (Catelyn's seventh chapter in ASOS — the Red Wedding)
22	- `affc-prologue.md` (prologues/epilogues use that as the name)
23	
24	D&E novellas use `collection: tales-of-dunk-and-egg` in frontmatter and `first_available: pre-agot` for spoiler gating (set pre-AGOT, ~90 years before the main series).
25	
26	### Extraction Files
27	Same name as source chapter with `.extraction.md` suffix:
28	- `agot-bran-01.extraction.md`
29	
30	### Node Files
31	`{entity-name-kebab-case}.node.md`
32	- `jaqen-hghar.node.md`
33	- `horn-of-joramun.node.md`
34	- `the-citadel.node.md`
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Entity Type Hierarchy
39	
40	Every node in the graph belongs to a type in this hierarchy. Child types inherit from their parent — a query for "organizations" returns houses, factions, and religions. A query for "events" returns both wars and individual battles.
41	
42	This hierarchy is **extensible**. New leaf types can be added without restructuring; new intermediate types should be rare and require design review.
43	
44	```
45	Entity
46	├── Character
47	│   ├── Human
48	│   ├── Direwolf          (Ghost, Grey Wind, Lady, Nymeria, Summer, Shaggydog)
49	│   └── Dragon            (Drogon, Rhaegal, Viserion; also historical: Balerion, Vhagar)
50	├── Place
51	│   ├── Location          (specific: Winterfell, Tower of Joy, The Citadel)
52	│   └── Region            (geographic: The North, Dorne, The Reach, Beyond the Wall)
53	├── Organization
54	│   ├── House             (sigil, words, seat, bloodline: House Stark, House Frey)
55	│   ├── Faction           (Night's Watch, Faceless Men, Golden Company, Brotherhood Without Banners)
56	│   └── Religion          (deities, clergy, rituals, sacred sites: Faith of the Seven, R'hllor, Old Gods)
57	├── Concept
58	│   ├── Culture           (customs, language, appearance norms: Dothraki, Ironborn, Free Folk)
59	│   ├── Magic             (rules, practitioners, costs: warging, greensight, shadowbinding, wildfire)
60	│   ├── Prophecy          (Azor Ahai, Maggy the Frog, House of the Undying visions)
61	│   └── Theory            (R+L=J, Grand Northern Conspiracy, Eldritch Apocalypse)
62	├── Object
63	│   ├── Artifact          (Valyrian steel swords, horns, crowns: Ice, Dragonbinder, Dawn)
64	│   └── Text              (in-world books, letters, songs: The Jade Compendium, The Rains of Castamere)
65	├── Event
66	│   ├── Battle            (single engagement: Red Wedding, Battle of the Blackwater)
67	│   ├── War               (multi-battle conflict: Robert's Rebellion, War of the Five Kings)
68	│   └── Tournament        (formal tourney: Tourney at Harrenhal, Hand's Tourney, Ashford Tourney)
69	├── Species               (biological type, NOT individual: dragons-as-species, Others, Children of the Forest)
70	├── Title                 (formal office: Hand of the King, Lord Commander, High Septon)
71	└── Meta                  (out-of-universe constructs — chapters, novels, publication metadata)
72	    ├── Chapter           (a chapter of a published novel: AGOT Chapter 1, ASOS Chapter 71)
73	    └── (future)          (Book, PovArc — added when needed)
74	```
75	
76	**Meta vs in-world** — Meta entities are out-of-universe (they describe the books *about* the world, not things *in* the world). They are categorically distinct from Event entities (Red Wedding, Battle of the Blackwater), which are in-world happenings. A `meta.chapter` node represents the literary container; the in-world events that happen *within* a chapter remain their own `event.battle/war/tournament` nodes. Edges from in-world entities to `meta.chapter` nodes are *citation/provenance* edges (this entity is featured in / discussed in this chapter), not in-world relationships.
77
